allSources.name: openbrewerydb/openbrewerydb
allSources.license: MIT
allSources.coverage: global; ~11,745 breweries, 79% field completeness
allSources.freshness: actively maintained; README stats refreshed June 2026
allSources.quality: high — normalized schema (name, brewery_type, address, city, state_province, postal_code, country, lat/long, phone, website_url)
allSources.notes: Formats: breweries.csv / breweries.json / breweries.sql. THE commercial-safe brewery backbone. Community-maintained via PRs.

allSources.name: Open Brewery DB — hosted REST API
allSources.license: MIT (Laravel API repo)
allSources.coverage: global (mirrors the dataset)
allSources.freshness: live/verified July 2026; Laravel API repo v1.1.0 dated May 2026
allSources.quality: high — 15-field JSON, full-text search (Meilisearch), filtering/pagination, /random endpoint
allSources.notes: No API key, no sign-up, no documented rate limit. Docs at api.openbrewerydb.org/docs. Backing repo: github.com/openbrewerydb/openbrewerydb-laravel-api (MIT). The older openbrewerydb-rails-api is DEPRECATED.

allSources.name: jsuchal/beer.db (+ openbeer org: world, us-united-states, by-bayern, etc.)
allSources.license: Public Domain (explicit dedication, no restrictions)
allSources.coverage: global, organized by country/region (~27 repos across the openbeer org)
allSources.quality: moderate — plain-text fixtures (beers, breweries, tags, taggings); record counts not stated
allSources.notes: openbeer.github.io is the project site. Truly unrestricted resale. Good supplemental global brewery/beer facts. jsuchal/beer.db is the original schema repo (public domain).

allSources.name: beerjson/beerjson
allSources.license: MIT
allSources.coverage: spec/standard (not a data dump)
allSources.freshness: v1.0.2 released Oct 2021; actively maintained via CI
allSources.quality: high — JSON Schema + TypeScript defs + XSD; supports detailed hop oil content, culture flocculation, pof, glucoamylase, maltster spec-sheet fields
allSources.notes: 162 stars. The interchange FORMAT for recipes/ingredients (successor to BeerXML 2). NPM @beerjson/beerjson. Use as product data model; contains schema not populated ingredient DB.

allSources.name: beerjson/bjcp-json
allSources.license: MIT
allSources.coverage: BJCP 2015, 2017, 2021 style guidelines in JSON
allSources.quality: high — machine-readable style categories + numeric ranges (ABV/IBU/SRM/OG/FG)
allSources.notes: CAVEAT: repo/code is MIT but BJCP Style Guideline PROSE is BJCP copyright. Names + numeric ranges are facts (safe); verify verbatim description reuse before selling. Alt: github.com/lrdodge/bjcp-style-data (MIT, multiple formats), bjcp-brasil/styleguide-2021 (JSON/XML/YAML), jrdn91/bjcp-guidelines-2021 (JSON + TS types).

allSources.name: kasperg3/HopDatabase
allSources.license: MIT
allSources.coverage: 500+ hop varieties, global
allSources.quality: high — alpha/beta acids, essential oils, cohumulone, aroma descriptors, country of origin, source attribution; JSON
allSources.notes: Scraped from hop-supplier spec sheets: Hopsteiner, Yakima Chief Hops, BarthHaas, Crosby Hops. MIT-licensed repo; underlying values are published spec-sheet facts (low IP risk). Cleanest commercial-usable HOPS ingredient source.

allSources.name: stuartraetaylor/hops-json
allSources.license: unclear — verify LICENSE file
allSources.coverage: hop varieties (JSON)
allSources.quality: moderate — hop database in JSON
allSources.notes: Secondary hops source; confirm license before commercial use. Use kasperg3/HopDatabase as primary.

allSources.name: almet/hops-datasets
allSources.license: unclear — verify
allSources.coverage: hop varieties: oils, lineage, age
allSources.quality: moderate — cohumulone %, caryophyllene, farnesene, geraniol, humulene, myrcene percentages
allSources.notes: Chemistry-detail hops data (aroma-oil breakdown). Good enrichment layer; verify license.

allSources.name: sboulema/BrewDB
allSources.license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
allSources.coverage: hops + malts + yeast ingredient tables
allSources.quality: high — SQLite; hops (usage/aromas/substitutions/aliases), malts (maltster/EBC/yield/protein), yeast (lab/temp/attenuation/flocculation/strain)
allSources.notes: WARNING: ShareAlike is a poison pill for a proprietary/resold product — derivatives must be re-licensed CC-BY-SA-4.0. Usable for internal enrichment, risky if the sold DB must stay proprietary. Prefer kasperg3 (MIT) for hops.

allSources.name: nickhould/craft-beers-dataset (Kaggle: craft-cans)
allSources.license: unknown/unset (Kaggle mirror license unclear)
allSources.coverage: US only; ~2,410 canned craft beers, ~558 breweries
allSources.freshness: static, older; source site CraftCans.com now defunct
allSources.quality: moderate — CSV (beers.csv, breweries.csv): name, ABV, IBU, style, ounces, brewery, city, state
allSources.notes: Scraped from CraftCans.com. Facts (name/ABV/IBU/style) low-risk but provenance/license weak. Good IPA/craft SEED or enrichment layer, not a clean licensed primary source. Kaggle: kaggle.com/datasets/nickhould/craft-cans.

allSources.name: brewdega/open-beer-database-dumps
allSources.license: verify (derived from Open Beer Database, historically open)
allSources.coverage: global; legacy Open Beer Database dump
allSources.quality: moderate — cleaned/sanitized beers.csv + breweries dumps
allSources.notes: Sanitized dump of the original openbeerdb.com project. Useful legacy beers.csv seed; verify license lineage before commercial reuse. Overlaps with beer.db lineage.

allSources.name: alxiw/punkapi (Punk API v3 / BrewDog DIY Dog)
allSources.license: MIT (wrapper) — but DATA is BrewDog IP
allSources.coverage: 415 BrewDog beers
allSources.freshness: current replacement (original PunkAPI discontinued May 2024); ~30 commits
allSources.quality: high — JSON: ABV, IBU, EBC, hops/malt/yeast w/ quantities, food pairing, brew methods/temps; extracted from BrewDog DIY Dog v8 PDF
allSources.notes: FLAG: MIT on wrapper does NOT license BrewDog's proprietary recipes/branding; DIY Dog is a single commercial brand's catalog. Use as reference for ingredient/recipe STRUCTURE, not a resellable data asset. punkapi.com original service is dead.

allSources.name: philipperemy/beer-dataset
allSources.license: none specified
allSources.coverage: ~30,280 beers
allSources.freshness: scraped late 2019; STALE; source (BreweryDB) shut down Jan 2020
allSources.quality: rich fields (ABV, IBU, SRM color, glassware, availability, style flavor profiles, label images) but tainted
allSources.notes: FLAG: scraped from BreweryDB (dead + pro-account ToS). No license. Do NOT commercialize. Same taint applies to aedobbyn/beer-data-science (also BreweryDB-derived) and raybuhr/w210_beer_demand (hops-brewerydb.csv).

allSources.name: SNAP Stanford — BeerAdvocate & RateBeer review corpora
allSources.license: SNAP code BSD; DATA withdrawn at BeerAdvocate's request
allSources.coverage: ~1.5M BeerAdvocate reviews + ~3M RateBeer reviews (through Nov 2011)
allSources.quality: high research quality — 5-aspect ratings (appearance/aroma/palate/taste/overall)
allSources.notes: RESEARCH-ONLY. Data pulled from public download at BeerAdvocate's request; academic-access only. The ONLY large ratings corpus but legally unusable commercially. Confirms: no clean open ratings source exists — biggest data gap for a monetizable ratings layer.

allSources.name: Beer rating/review scrapers (BeerAdvocate / RateBeer / Untappd)
allSources.license: varies (code only)
allSources.coverage: whatever the target sites hold
allSources.freshness: tools; output depends on run date
allSources.quality: n/a — code, not data
allSources.notes: FLAG scrape-only + ToS-prohibited. Includes glederrey/ratebeer_crawler + glederrey/beeradvocate_crawler, sbuss/beeradvocate-scraper, vavlani/beer-scrape, zongqi-wang/Beer-Advocate-Scraper, harrisiirak/ratebeer-scraper, hadeli/beer-crawler, akionakamura/untappd-scraper, rikunert/beer_rating (R, multi-site). Untappd/BeerAdvocate/RateBeer ToS forbid scraping + commercial reuse; content owned by reviewers/sites. Do NOT use for a commercial product.

allSources.name: davestroud/BeerStudy
allSources.license: unclear
allSources.coverage: US; 2,410 craft beers, 558 breweries
allSources.quality: moderate — same underlying CraftCans-derived data as nickhould set
allSources.notes: Duplicate lineage of the craft-cans dataset. Marginal incremental value; verify license.

allSources.name: Open Brewery DB
allSources.coverage: global but US-heavy; ~11,745 breweries across 14 countries (US ~70%, Germany ~12%); brewpubs/cideries/bottleshops included; NO individual beers/reviews
allSources.freshness: Dataset last updated June 2026; community-maintained via GitHub, weekly-ish updates. Originally scraped from Brewers Association ~Aug 2018.
allSources.license: MIT (code + dataset; LICENSE file Copyright 2025 Open Brewery DB). FAQ: dataset 'will always be free and open-source'; 'welcome to use the dataset for any projects with credit to Open Brewery DB.'
allSources.quality: Good structured brewery/location data; ~79% field completeness. Type taxonomy (micro, brewpub, regional, contract, planning, etc.).
allSources.notes: LIVE. Endpoints (v1, no auth, no documented hard rate limit but be polite): GET /v1/breweries (list+filter by_city/by_state/by_country/by_type/by_dist), /v1/breweries/{obdb-id}, /v1/breweries/random, /v1/breweries/search?query=, /v1/breweries/meta. Bulk CSV + PostgreSQL SQL + JSON downloads on GitHub — USE THESE for commercial/production (self-host) rather than the community API (maintainer explicitly discourages relying on the hosted API for production/SLA). Fields: id, name, brewery_type, address_1/2/3, city, state_province, postal_code, country, longitude, latitude, phone, website_url. Cleanest commercial anchor for the brewery layer. GitHub: https://github.com/openbrewerydb/openbrewerydb

allSources.name: Foursquare Open Source Places (FSQ OS Places)
allSources.coverage: global; 100M+ POIs worldwide across all categories — filter to beer categories (brewery, brewpub, beer bar, beer garden, taproom, bottle shop). No beer-product data, no ABV/styles.
allSources.freshness: Released GA Nov 2024; refreshed monthly. Actively maintained through 2026.
allSources.license: Apache License 2.0 — full commercial use, redistribution, monetization, and RESALE permitted; NO share-alike/copyleft. Attribution/NOTICE file requirement only.
allSources.quality: Foundational POI dataset; 22+ core attributes (name, address, region/city/country, lat/lng, category, website, social handles, hours where available). Venue-grade, not beer-catalog-grade.
allSources.notes: LIVE. Best worldwide VENUE/GEO layer with a fully resalable license. Distributed as Parquet/GeoParquet on Amazon S3 + Hugging Face (foursquare/fsq-os-places). Bulk download, not a live query API — you host/serve it. HF: https://huggingface.co/datasets/foursquare/fsq-os-places . Pair with Open Brewery DB for brewery identity + FSQ for bars/taprooms globally.

allSources.name: Overture Maps Foundation — Places theme
allSources.coverage: global; ~60M+ POIs conflated from Meta, Microsoft, Foursquare, PinMeTo, RenderSEO, AllThePlaces, etc. Filter by category to beer venues. No beer-product data.
allSources.freshness: Monthly releases (e.g., 2025-09 added ~6M POIs from FSQ OS Places). Actively maintained 2026; backed by Linux Foundation (AWS/Meta/Microsoft/TomTom).
allSources.license: Per-row by source: CDLA Permissive 2.0 (Meta, Microsoft, PinMeTo, Krick, RenderSEO, DAC, BrightQuery), Apache 2.0 (Foursquare), CC0 1.0 (AllThePlaces). ALL permissive — commercial redistribution, monetization, and resale allowed; NO copyleft/share-alike. Minimal attribution.
allSources.quality: Production-grade, deduplicated/conflated POIs with stable GERS IDs — cleaner than raw single-source POI dumps.
allSources.notes: LIVE. Download via AWS S3 / Azure (Registry of Open Data on AWS: https://registry.opendata.aws/overture/) or Overture CLI as GeoParquet. Bulk data, self-host. Strong resalable alternative/complement to FSQ OS Places for the venue layer. Attribution page: https://docs.overturemaps.org/attribution/

allSources.name: Catalog.beer
allSources.coverage: US-centric (addresses/location search US-only; intl brewers addable with country code). ~60,604 beers + ~6,760 brewers.
allSources.freshness: Live and community-editable (CRUD endpoints let users add/verify data). Actively serving in 2026.
allSources.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0) — commercial use and resale permitted WITH mandatory attribution; excludes breweries' name/brand/trademark rights. Attribution is a real obligation to design around in a sold product.
allSources.quality: One of very few LIVE sources with actual per-BEER attributes (name, style, ABV, IBU, description) + nested brewer object. US-focused, crowd-editable so completeness/accuracy varies.
allSources.notes: LIVE. Auth = API key via HTTP Basic (key as username, blank password, base64). Free tier = 1,000 requests/month (resets 1st of month); higher volume by contacting them (cost-recovery pricing). Endpoints: brewers, beers, locations (find by coordinates/ZIP/city), users, usage — full CRUD + search. Best available commercially-licensed BEER catalog (vs brewery-only sources), but confirm CC BY attribution mechanics are acceptable for a resold/monetized dataset. API usage/pricing: https://catalog.beer/api-usage

allSources.name: OpenStreetMap (breweries/bars via Overpass API + planet extracts)
allSources.coverage: global; breweries via craft=brewery, microbrewery=yes, industrial=brewery; bars/pubs via amenity=bar/pub. Coverage uneven by region (strong in Europe). No beer-product data.
allSources.freshness: Continuously updated (real-time edits). Overpass = live queries; planet/Geofabrik extracts refreshed daily/weekly.
allSources.license: Open Database License (ODbL 1.0) — commercial use ALLOWED but SHARE-ALIKE/copyleft: any publicly distributed derived DATABASE must be offered under ODbL, plus attribution. This is the licensing catch for a 'sold/monetized' product — OK for internal enrichment or ODbL-licensed outputs, risky if you want a proprietary resalable dataset.
allSources.quality: Good geo + tags (name, website, brewery, opening_hours), variable completeness. brewmap.openstreetmap.de + openbeermap.github.io visualize it.
allSources.notes: LIVE. Query via Overpass API / Overpass Turbo, or bulk via Geofabrik extracts. FLAG: ODbL share-alike makes clean resale of a derived proprietary DB legally awkward — treat as reference/enrichment, not the resale core, unless you accept ODbL on outputs. OpenBeerMap: https://openbeermap.github.io/

allSources.name: Foursquare Places API (commercial / PAYG)
allSources.coverage: global POIs incl. beer venues; richer live attributes (tips, hours, popularity, photos) than the OS dataset.
allSources.freshness: Live, continuously updated managed API. New Places API/V2 Pro endpoint rates effective June 1, 2026 (500 free Pro calls included).
allSources.license: Commercial API License Agreement (PAYG) — usage-based; permits use in your service but RESTRICTS resale/redistribution of raw Places Data: you must make 'commercially reasonable efforts' to prevent your service's Places Data from being crawled; contractual anti-crawl terms on your end users required. NOT for reselling the underlying data as a dataset.
allSources.quality: High — managed, enriched, deduplicated venue data + live signals.
allSources.notes: LIVE, PAID. Good as a live lookup/enrichment layer inside the product, but its ToS blocks reselling the raw data as a transferable dataset. For a data-monetization/resale business, prefer FSQ OS Places (Apache 2.0) over this API. Terms: https://foursquare.com/legal/terms/apilicenseagreement/ ; Pricing: https://foursquare.com/pricing/

allSources.name: Google Places API (New)
allSources.coverage: global; best-in-class venue coverage/freshness for breweries, brewpubs, beer bars incl. ratings, hours, photos, reviews.
allSources.freshness: Live, constantly updated. Per-SKU billing (the flat $200 monthly credit was retired ~2025; now per-SKU free tiers).
allSources.license: Google Maps Platform ToS — HIGHLY RESTRICTIVE for a data business: no permanent storage of most Places content (limited caching; only place_id may be stored long-term, content must be refreshed ~every 30 days), no pre-fetching/building/creating a database or dataset from the content, and no using the data to build/train/improve a competing dataset or product. Effectively NON-transferable / non-resalable.
allSources.quality: Excellent live data quality, but legally a dead end for resale.
allSources.notes: LIVE, PAID. 2026 indicative pricing per 1,000: Place Details Pro ~$17; Text/Nearby Search Pro ~$32; Enterprise ~$35; Enterprise+Atmosphere (reviews, etc.) ~$40. USE ONLY as a runtime lookup you don't persist/resell. FLAG: prohibits building a stored/derived database — incompatible with 'data monetized and sold.' Pricing analysis: https://openplacesapi.com/blog/google-places-api-pricing

allSources.name: Untappd API (v4 public + Untappd for Business)
allSources.coverage: global; the largest beer + check-in/rating + venue taplist graph (huge beer catalog w/ styles/ABV, ratings, venues).
allSources.freshness: Live and current; the richest live beer dataset. Public API v4 still exists in 2026 via approval form (~2 business days); UFB API is Premium-subscriber-only.
allSources.license: API ToS EXPRESSLY PROHIBITS commercial data use: 'You will not sell, rent, or trade any data acquired from the Untappd API'; 'You cannot use the Untappd data to build your own beer database'; no analytics/mining; MUST delete caches every 24 hours; no mass marketing. Rate limit 100 calls/hour/key.
allSources.quality: Best-in-class beer/rating/venue data — but legally unusable for this project.
allSources.notes: LIVE but DO-NOT-USE for a commercialized/resalable product. FLAG: cache-purge-every-24h + no-resale + no-own-database clauses make it incompatible with data monetization and transfer. Owned by Next Glass. Scraping Untappd (e.g., Apify 'Untappd Scraper') also violates ToS — avoid. Public API docs: https://untappd.com/api/docs ; UFB: https://docs.business.untappd.com/

allSources.name: beer.db / Open Beer Database (openbeer)
allSources.coverage: global-ish, patchy; plain-text (CSV) fixtures for beers, breweries, brewpubs + beer maps (Austria, Bayern, Belgium, Czechia, etc.).
allSources.freshness: STALE — repos last meaningful commits early 2024 (some assets 2023). Effectively dormant/community-archived in 2026. Verify before depending on it.
allSources.license: PUBLIC DOMAIN — 'schema, data and scripts are dedicated to the public domain. Use it as you please with no restrictions whatsoever.' Cleanest possible license for commercial use + resale (public-domain, like CC0).
allSources.quality: Structured (name, ABV, style, brewery, geo) but modest size and outdated; good as seed/reference data, not a live source.
allSources.notes: Cleanest LICENSE of any beer-product source, but the data is old. Best use: one-time public-domain seed for beer/style reference tables, then maintain yourself. GitHub org: https://github.com/openbeer ; tools: https://github.com/beerkit/beer.db . jsuchal mirror: https://github.com/jsuchal/beer.db

allSources.name: Punk API v3 (BrewDog DIY Dog — alxiw fork)
allSources.coverage: single-brewery: all 415 BrewDog DIY Dog recipes only. Rich per-beer detail (ABV, IBU, EBC, SRM, malts, hops, yeast, food pairings, brewing tips, image). NOT a market catalog.
allSources.freshness: Original punkapi.com by Sam Mason SHUT DOWN May 1, 2024 (domain now dead — punkapi.com does not resolve). alxiw fork is the maintained successor, live at https://punkapi-alxiw.amvera.io/v3/ (self-hosted; small project, no SLA).
allSources.license: Fork CODE is MIT. Underlying DATA = BrewDog's 'DIY Dog' recipe book, which BrewDog released free 'to the homebrew community' — the source data's commercial-use/redistribution status is NOT clearly licensed for resale. FLAG as unclear for a sold product.
allSources.quality: High detail but tiny scope (one brewery's recipes); useful as demo/sample or homebrew-recipe reference, not core catalog.
allSources.notes: Original DEAD; fork LIVE. Endpoints mirror v2: /v3/beers, /v3/beers/{id}, /v3/beers/random (pagination via page/per_page). Treat as a niche recipe dataset; get clarity on DIY Dog reuse rights before monetizing. Wrappers often bundle offline data as fallback.

allSources.name: WineVybe Beer API (via RapidAPI)
allSources.coverage: claims 'beers from all over the globe'; beers + breweries, ABV, descriptions, food pairing, awards, classifications (beers/malts/ales), packaging dims/weights. Unverified size.
allSources.freshness: Live on RapidAPI marketplace in 2026; provenance and update cadence undisclosed.
allSources.license: Not clearly stated. WineVybe explicitly 'does not claim ownership of any of the images or data provided by the API' — meaning THEY don't grant you rights to underlying third-party data/images; resale rights are unclear and likely limited by the original (unnamed) sources. FLAG.
allSources.quality: Unknown/opaque — no named data source; treat with caution. Sister 'beer9'/Liquor APIs on same account.
allSources.notes: LIVE, PAID (~$0.02/request; free RapidAPI account to start). Auth via RapidAPI (OAuth2/OpenID). FLAG for commercialization: undisclosed data provenance + explicit no-ownership disclaimer = weak/unclear resale rights. Do diligence before relying on it. RapidAPI: https://rapidapi.com/winevybe/api/beer9

allSources.name: BreweryDB.com (PintLabs)
allSources.coverage: was the largest structured beer+brewery+style catalog (US-strong).
allSources.freshness: DEAD for self-serve. Public API discontinued (wound down ~2020); site now states no public API access — data-partnership inquiries only (case-by-case, not open).
allSources.license: N/A publicly — no self-serve terms; any access is a private negotiated partnership.
allSources.quality: Historically excellent, but inaccessible now.
allSources.notes: CONFIRMED not available as a live self-serve API in 2026. Listed so it's explicitly ruled out (this is the classic 'dead beer API'). Only route is contacting them for a private data partnership; do not design around it. Historical dumps floating on GitHub are stale and of uncertain license — avoid for a sold product.

allSources.name: RateBeer API / data
allSources.coverage: was a large beer + expert-review corpus (owned by ZX Ventures / AB InBev).
allSources.freshness: DEAD — RateBeer.com shut down Feb 1, 2025; API had been down/approval-only well before. Site and API effectively gone in 2026.
allSources.license: Former API required pre-approved licensee agreement (restricted). Review corpus is proprietary AB InBev data — never open/commercially licensable to third parties.
allSources.quality: Historically strong reviews, now inaccessible.
allSources.notes: CONFIRMED DEAD (shutdown Feb 2025). FLAG as a restricted/defunct review corpus — do not rely on it. Any scraped RateBeer data is ToS-violating and AB-InBev-owned; unusable for resale. Included to explicitly rule out. Shutdown coverage: https://www.forbes.com/sites/dontse/2025/01/22/why-ratebeer-is-shutting-down-and-the-person-trying-to-save-it/

allSources.name: TapHunter → Evergreen (taplist/menu data)
allSources.coverage: US-focused draft/taplist + beverage menu data; historically a 300,000+ entry beverage database + venue taplists.
allSources.freshness: TapHunter acquired by and absorbed into Evergreen (evergreenhq.com); the standalone public JSON website-menu feed is legacy. No open public API in 2026.
allSources.license: No public/open data license; it's a B2B SaaS for breweries/bars. Any data access would be a commercial contract, and it's their customers' menu data — resale rights not offered.
allSources.quality: Good live taplist/menu data, but locked inside a SaaS, not available as an open feed.
allSources.notes: No self-serve API for third-party data resale. Legacy TapHunter 'Website Menu JSON feed' was for a venue to embed its OWN menu, not a bulk data source. Contact Evergreen for partnership; do not assume resale rights. BeerMenus (competitor) explicitly has NO public API (help.beermenus.com/article/44).

allSources.name: Open Brewery DB (GitHub dataset + API)
allSources.coverage: global (23 countries), ~11,745 breweries; ~79% field completeness
allSources.freshness: Last full dataset update 2024; stats regenerated 2026-06-28; community-maintained via PRs
allSources.license: MIT
allSources.quality: Good, curated, actively maintained; brewery-level only (no per-beer ABV/IBU/style)
allSources.notes: Fields: id, name, brewery_type, street, city, state_province, postal_code, country, longitude, latitude, phone, website_url. Formats: CSV, JSON, PostgreSQL SQL. Companion JSON API at api.openbrewerydb.org has no key/rate limit. THE cleanest resale-safe brewery source. Does NOT contain a beer catalog.

allSources.name: TTB Public COLA Registry (live)
allSources.coverage: US — effectively every alcohol beverage legally sold in the US; COLAs 1999–present (some pre-1996 partial)
allSources.freshness: Continuous; new approvals appear ~48h after approval
allSources.license: US Government public domain (CC0 for the data.gov mirror)
allSources.quality: Authoritative federal registry; fields include brand name, fanciful name, class/type (style), applicant/brewer name+address, ABV (on many), serial number, approval/expiry dates, and scanned label images. No IBU or tasting profiles.
allSources.notes: No registration to search/view. Bulk download is via the registry's search-and-download UI (no clean bulk API) — build a compliant extractor. Public-domain data is resale-safe. Best single source for the US beer universe + ABV + label art.

allSources.name: TTB COLA Registry snapshot (data.gov)
allSources.coverage: US COLA extract
allSources.freshness: STALE — dataset frozen ~2014-02-06; catalog entry last checked 2025-08
allSources.license: CC0 1.0 (US public domain)
allSources.quality: Public-domain but badly outdated; use only as a historical baseline. Prefer the live registry above.
allSources.notes: Confirms the CC0/public-domain status of COLA data. HTML/interface access.

allSources.name: TTB Beer Statistics (Beer Reports)
allSources.coverage: US national/state production, removals, excise volumes
allSources.freshness: Monthly + yearly releases from Brewer's Report of Operations (TTB F 5130.9 / 5130.26)
allSources.license: US Government public domain
allSources.quality: Aggregate industry stats (production/tax volumes), NOT a per-beer catalog. Good for market-sizing features.
allSources.notes: Downloadable report tables. Public-domain, resale-safe.

allSources.name: beer.db (openbeer / jsuchal)
allSources.coverage: Global sample; breweries with founding year+location, beers with ABV+style, brands, country
allSources.freshness: Low activity / older data; community fixtures
allSources.license: Public Domain dedication ('use as you please, no restrictions whatsoever')
allSources.quality: Modest coverage but the licensing is the cleanest possible (explicit public-domain). Plain-text CSV fixtures, buildable into SQL.
allSources.notes: Also see jsuchal/beer.db (https://github.com/jsuchal/beer.db) and site https://openbeer.github.io/. Great resale-safe seed for beer+brewery+style with ABV; no IBU/tasting profiles.

allSources.name: BrewDog DIY Dog + Punk API v3 (community)
allSources.coverage: 415 BrewDog beers (DIY Dog v8); IPA-heavy
allSources.freshness: DIY Dog v8 (2019 catalogue); API re-hosted after original Punk API retired May 2024
allSources.license: API code MIT; underlying DIY Dog recipe DATA released free by BrewDog for homebrewers (no explicit resale grant)
allSources.quality: High-quality, fully-specified beer records: name, tagline, description, ABV, IBU, EBC, SRM, target OG/FG, pH, attenuation, mash/fermentation method, hops+malt+yeast with amounts, food pairings, brewer's tips, image. JSON + PNG.
allSources.notes: Excellent IPA-specific seed set. MIT covers the code, NOT necessarily the right to resell BrewDog's data — safe for enrichment/prototyping; get written permission before selling the raw corpus. Original punkapi.com (v2) discontinued; source PDF: BrewDog DIY Dog V8 (brewdogmedia.s3...2019+DIY+DOG+-+V8.pdf), also drink.brewdog.com/uk/diy-dog with BeerXML downloads.

allSources.name: Kaggle — Brewer's Friend Beer Recipes (jtrofe)
allSources.coverage: ~75,000 homebrew recipes
allSources.license: Listed on Kaggle (verify current license field) — data scraped from brewersfriend.com
allSources.quality: Rich recipe fields: Style, StyleID, Size, OG, FG, ABV, IBU, Color(SRM), BoilSize/Time, Efficiency, Mash thickness, Sugar scale, Brew method, Pitch rate, Primary temp. CSV.
allSources.notes: Great for style/ABV/IBU/recipe ML. Provenance is scraped homebrew recipes — fine for prototyping; re-verify Kaggle license + Brewer's Friend ToS before reselling derived data.

allSources.name: Kaggle — Homebrew Beer Recipes / larger Brewer's Friend set
allSources.coverage: 180k+ homebrew recipes (larger variant)
allSources.license: Kaggle-listed (verify); scraped from Brewer's Friend
allSources.quality: Same style/ABV/IBU/OG/FG recipe schema as above, larger volume.
allSources.notes: See also Compubeer's 400,000+ BeerXML corpus (https://compubeer.net) for an even larger homebrew set. Scraped provenance — prototyping/ML, not clean resale.

allSources.name: Kaggle — Beer Profile and Ratings (ruthgn)
allSources.coverage: ~3,197 beers with tasting-profile vectors
allSources.license: Kaggle-listed; DERIVED FROM BeerAdvocate reviews
allSources.quality: Unique flavor/aroma/mouthfeel numeric profiles + Name, Style, Brewery, ABV, Min/Max IBU, Astringency, Body, Alcohol, Bitter, Sweet, Sour, Salty, Fruits, Hoppy, Spices, Malty, plus review scores.
allSources.notes: Very useful tasting-profile features BUT upstream is BeerAdvocate (whose data was pulled from SNAP at their request). Treat as research/prototyping; do NOT resell derived tasting data without clearing BeerAdvocate rights.

allSources.name: Kaggle — Craft Beers Dataset (nickhould / craft-cans)
allSources.coverage: 2,410 US canned craft beers, 510 breweries
allSources.freshness: Static (~2017, scraped from CraftCans.com which is itself defunct)
allSources.license: Kaggle-listed (commonly cited as open; verify) — scraped from CraftCans.com
allSources.quality: Clean small catalog: beers.csv (abv, ibu, id, name, style, brewery_id, ounces) + breweries.csv (name, city, state). Also mirrored at github.com/nickhould/craft-beers-dataset.
allSources.notes: Popular teaching dataset with real ABV+IBU+style. Small and dated. Scraped provenance — verify before resale.

allSources.name: UCSD/McAuley RecSys Datasets — BeerAdvocate & RateBeer reviews
allSources.coverage: BeerAdvocate ~1.5M reviews; RateBeer ~3M reviews (recrawled version extends to Aug 2017); 5 aspects each (appearance, aroma, palate, taste, overall)
allSources.license: Academic/research use only; RateBeer (RelBench mirror) explicitly CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (NON-COMMERCIAL)
allSources.quality: The canonical large beer-review NLP corpora — excellent for models, radioactive for a sellable product.
allSources.notes: RESEARCH-ONLY. Flagged per instructions. Do not embed in a commercialized/monetized product.

allSources.name: SNAP Stanford — Web data: BeerAdvocate / RateBeer reviews
allSources.coverage: BeerAdvocate 1.59M reviews, 33,387 users, 66,051 beers (1998–2011); RateBeer counterpart at snap.stanford.edu/data/web-RateBeer.html
allSources.license: Research use; BeerAdvocate download REMOVED at BeerAdvocate's request
allSources.quality: Historically important; now DE-LISTED ('As per request from BeerAdvocate the data is no longer available').
allSources.notes: Strong signal of the legal risk around review corpora. Non-commercial and no longer distributed. Avoid for a sellable app.

allSources.name: data.world — BeerAdvocate (socialmediadata) & beer collection
allSources.coverage: ~38 beer datasets incl. socialmediadata/beeradvocate (BeerAdvocate 1.5M reviews mirror)
allSources.license: Mixed/per-dataset; BeerAdvocate mirror inherits BeerAdvocate restrictions (non-commercial)
allSources.quality: Aggregator; convenient but the community/open-data portal is being retired 2026-07-11.
allSources.notes: URGENT: data.world Open Data Community closes 2026-07-11 — download anything needed before then. BeerAdvocate mirror is not resale-safe.

allSources.name: philipperemy/beer-dataset (BreweryDB scrape)
allSources.coverage: 30,280 beers
allSources.freshness: Scraped late 2019 from the now-defunct BreweryDB
allSources.license: NONE stated
allSources.quality: Rich fields (name, style, ABV, IBU, description, brewery, glass, SRM/color, availability, serving temp, label images, timestamps) BUT unlicensed scrape of a shut-down API.
allSources.notes: BreweryDB shut down 2020. No license = legally unusable for a sellable product despite being technically downloadable. Listed as a WARNING, not a recommendation.

allSources.name: Brewers Association — Official Brewery Directory
allSources.coverage: Most comprehensive US + intl brewery list; independent-craft seal flags
allSources.license: Proprietary; no public bulk download/API; member-benefit data
allSources.quality: Authoritative but locked — web directory only, no license to extract/resell.
allSources.notes: Industry data sets (production, excise) at brewersassociation.org/statistics-and-data are member-gated. Not a resale-safe source; contact BA for licensing.

allSources.name: Zenodo — Beer Ontology
allSources.coverage: Ontology for beer + ingredient traceability, process control, style identification
allSources.license: Zenodo record license (typically CC — verify on record; commonly CC-BY)
allSources.quality: A schema/ontology, NOT a records dataset — useful for modeling styles/ingredients relationships, not for populating a catalog.
allSources.notes: Good complement for taxonomy/normalization of styles and ingredients.

allSources.name: HuggingFace — trungpq/beer-com-reviews & arize-ai beer_reviews_label_drift
allSources.coverage: Beer.com / BeerAdvocate-style review sets (multi-aspect ratings 0–5)
allSources.license: Per-dataset on HF (verify card); review provenance typically restricted
allSources.quality: Convenient ML-ready review data; arize-ai/beer_reviews_label_drift_neutral is a drift-monitoring slice.
allSources.notes: Check each dataset card's license; review-derived data carries the same non-commercial risk as the source. Prototyping/ML, not resale.

allSources.name: OpenStreetMap via Overpass API + Geofabrik extracts
allSources.coverage: global; craft=brewery ~11,388 objects, microbrewery=yes ~7,958, amenity=pub ~198,654, amenity=bar ~242,091 (taginfo 2026-07-07)
allSources.freshness: Geofabrik daily; Overpass live but rate-limited (~10k req/day, <1GB/day)
allSources.license: ODbL 1.0
allSources.quality: High for EU/DACH + Anglosphere beer venues; volunteer-tagged so attribute completeness varies
allSources.notes: Commercial use AND resale permitted, BUT attribution to OpenStreetMap required and SHARE-ALIKE applies to derivative databases you publicly distribute. Internal use triggers no share-alike. Overpass NOT for bulk extraction — use Geofabrik static PBF/shapefiles for that. brewmap.openstreetmap.de renders these tags. Join key: OSM object id.

allSources.name: Foursquare Open Source (OS) Places
allSources.coverage: global; 100M+ POIs, 1000+ categories
allSources.license: Apache-2.0
allSources.quality: Broad global coverage; category precision and freshness vary vs Overture
allSources.notes: Fully resellable (Apache-2.0). Available via Hugging Face (foursquare/fsq-os-places), AWS, and Source Cooperative (Fused GeoParquet). Also a single 100M+ PMTiles file (github.com/wipfli/foursquare-os-places-pmtiles). Same Foursquare data also flows into Overture. Join key: fsq_id.

allSources.name: Wikidata — brewery entities (SPARQL)
allSources.coverage: global; ~1,806 breweries (instance/subclass of Q131734) with coordinate location P625
allSources.quality: Small but high-quality structured facts (name, website, founding date, parent company, logo)
allSources.notes: CC0 = no strings, fully resellable. Too small to be a base layer; ideal zero-obligation ENRICHMENT joined via QID. Query: P31/P279* Q131734 + P625.

allSources.name: Geoapify Places API
allSources.coverage: global; 800+ categories incl. catering.pub / catering.bar; built on OSM/open data
allSources.license: permissive commercial (provider terms allow storing/caching results, incl. commercially)
allSources.quality: Convenient OSM-derived API; ultimately OSM lineage so ODbL attribution prudence advised
allSources.notes: Free plan 3,000 credits/day (20 places = 1 credit), commercial use allowed within quota; explicitly permits storing/caching derived place data unlike Google. Useful as a managed query layer over OSM without self-hosting Overpass. Because data is OSM-derived, retain OSM attribution to be safe.

allSources.name: Apple MapKit — MKLocalPointsOfInterestRequest (.brewery)
allSources.coverage: global (Apple Maps POI); native to the target platform
allSources.license: Apple Developer Program License Agreement / Apple Maps Terms of Use
allSources.quality: High native quality and best UX fit for an Apple Maps feature — but unusable as stored data
allSources.notes: RUNTIME-ONLY. MKPointOfInterestCategory.brewery exists (iOS 13+); iOS 14+ POI-only search via init(request:). ToS FORBID caching/prefetch/storage beyond temporary perf, FORBID any secondary/derived database, FORBID bulk download/scraping/model training, and FORBID using data to build a competing map service. Use solely as an in-app live fallback; it can NEVER feed the monetized catalog.

allSources.name: Google Places API
allSources.coverage: global; very high POI quality
allSources.license: Google Maps Platform Service Specific Terms
allSources.quality: High data quality but legally incompatible with the use case
allSources.notes: DISQUALIFIED for a stored/resellable Apple Maps dataset: only place_id may be stored indefinitely; other content cannot be cached/stored; lat/lng cacheable max 30 days; and Places content must NOT be used with a non-Google map (fatal for an Apple Maps feature). No resale of derived data.

allSources.name: BreweryDB API
allSources.coverage: formerly global
allSources.license: n/a
allSources.quality: Defunct
allSources.notes: Public API no longer available (shut down ~2020). Superseded by Open Brewery DB. Do not build on it.

allSources.name: RateBeer
allSources.coverage: formerly global
allSources.license: proprietary ToS (scraping prohibited)
allSources.quality: Defunct
allSources.notes: Site ceased operations Feb 1 2025 (per BeerAdvocate community). ToS forbade crawling/scraping. Non-commercial, dead — avoid.

allSources.name: Untappd (for Business API)
allSources.coverage: global venues/menus
allSources.license: Untappd API Terms of Use
allSources.quality: Rich taplist/menu + rating data, but non-resellable
allSources.notes: Business Premium subscribers only. ToS explicitly: 'will not sell, rent, or trade any data acquired from the Untappd API'; no bulk marketing. Cannot feed a resold dataset. Flag as ToS-restricted.

allSources.name: BeerAdvocate
allSources.coverage: global reviews
allSources.license: proprietary ToS
allSources.quality: Large review corpus but legally off-limits
allSources.notes: ToS prohibits scraping and any unauthorized reuse of content. Research/editorial only — do NOT scrape or commercialize. Flag as scrape-forbidden / non-commercial.

allSources.name: BJCP Style Guidelines (official) + usage FAQ
allSources.license: © BJCP, all rights reserved; permission needed for reuse
allSources.coverage: global; ~100+ beer styles (2021), plus mead/cider
allSources.quality: authoritative, canonical homebrew/competition taxonomy
allSources.notes: FLAG: full descriptive prose is non-commercial ('resulting version must be provided for free if used in an app'). BUT explicitly allowed for reuse: 'Our naming and numbering system, the style parameters, and the overall impression section can be used for other purposes.' => Ingest numbers/names/ABV-IBU-SRM ranges only; write your own descriptions. Do not host full text.

allSources.name: beerjson/bjcp-json (BJCP styles as JSON in BeerJSON format)
allSources.license: MIT (repo code) — underlying BJCP/BA prose still BJCP/BA-copyright
allSources.coverage: BJCP 2015, BJCP 2021, BA 2017
allSources.quality: clean machine-readable style data
allSources.notes: Convenient JSON of styles, but the descriptive TEXT inherits BJCP/BA non-commercial constraints. Use structural fields (name, number, stats); avoid shipping full prose in a paid product.

allSources.name: BeerJSON specification (interchange schema)
allSources.license: MIT
allSources.coverage: global schema standard; docs at beerjson.github.io/beerjson
allSources.quality: the de-facto modern brewing data interchange format (successor to BeerXML)
allSources.notes: Use as your canonical schema for styles + ingredient objects (fermentable/hop/culture/misc types). MIT = fully commercial. Schema only — you supply the data.

allSources.name: Brewers Association Beer Style Guidelines
allSources.license: © Brewers Association; 'used with permission' attribution required
allSources.coverage: global; ~180+ competition styles; updated yearly (2026 PDF live)
allSources.quality: authoritative, GABF/World Beer Cup taxonomy
allSources.notes: HARD FLAG: 'Permission ... is not granted when the end use is for a cost (that is, not provided free).' Cannot ship in a paid/sellable product. Free-only, attribution required. 2026 PDF: cdn.brewersassociation.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/19140641/2026_BA_Beer_Style_Guidelines.pdf

allSources.name: lrdodge/bjcp-style-data (BJCP 2021 in JSON/XML/YAML)
allSources.license: repo tooling permissive; data = 'Copyright BJCP, used by permission'
allSources.coverage: BJCP 2021 beer styles
allSources.quality: multi-format, easy to ingest
allSources.notes: Same BJCP prose constraint. Good for style structure; not for reselling full descriptions.

allSources.name: Wikidata (beer & brewery entities)
allSources.license: CC0 1.0 (public domain, all structured data)
allSources.coverage: global; brewery items, beer brands (Q15075508 'beer brand'), styles; tens of thousands of relevant entities
allSources.freshness: live, continuously updated; SPARQL endpoint + full dumps
allSources.quality: canonical, cross-referenced; carries external IDs (Untappd, RateBeer, BeerAdvocate, Wikipedia, official website, logo image)
allSources.notes: BEST license possible (CC0 — royalty-free, irrevocable, commercial OK, no attribution required). Use as the canonical ID spine and to cross-link to other sources. Query via WDQS SPARQL; bulk via Wikidata dumps.

allSources.name: DBpedia (dbo:Beer / brewery ontology)
allSources.license: CC-BY-SA 3.0
allSources.coverage: global; Wikipedia-derived beer/brewery entities + abstracts
allSources.quality: good for descriptive abstracts and infobox facts
allSources.notes: CC-BY-SA = attribution + SHARE-ALIKE (viral). Wikidata (CC0) covers the same entities with a cleaner license — prefer Wikidata; use DBpedia only if you want CC-BY-SA prose abstracts and accept share-alike.

allSources.name: sboulema/BrewDB (SQLite brewing ingredients)
allSources.license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
allSources.coverage: hops (usage/aroma/subs/aliases), malts (maltster/EBC/yield/protein/diastatic power), yeast (lab/temp/attenuation/flocculation/alcohol tolerance)
allSources.quality: good structured multi-ingredient coverage; SQLite
allSources.notes: FLAG: CC-BY-SA-4.0 share-alike is VIRAL — derivatives must stay CC-BY-SA. Data also compiled from producer sheets. Prefer MIT HopDatabase for hops; if using BrewDB for malt/yeast, isolate it in a share-alike layer or re-derive facts.

allSources.name: Open Beer Database dumps (brewdega mirror)
allSources.license: ODbL + Database Contents License (Open Data Commons)
allSources.coverage: ~5,900 beers / ~1,400 breweries, category+style tables (legacy 2011 vintage)
allSources.quality: outdated for current beers; category/style lists still useful
allSources.notes: FLAG: ODbL share-alike is viral on the database. Fine for seeding style/category taxonomy in a separable attributed layer; do not treat as current beer data. Original site openbeerdb.com is defunct.

allSources.name: Open Food Facts (barcode/UPC → product)
allSources.license: database: ODbL; contents: DbCL; images: CC-BY-SA
allSources.coverage: ~36,900 alcoholic-beverage products (13,606 with nutrition), global; barcodes (EAN/UPC)
allSources.quality: variable/crowd-sourced; real barcodes; some label photos
allSources.notes: FLAG: ODbL SHARE-ALIKE (viral) on the DB and CC-BY-SA on images — a proprietary sellable DB derived from it must stay open/attributed. Usable if you keep OFF-derived data in a separable share-alike module. Label PHOTOS are user-uploaded and depict copyrighted/trademarked labels — CC-BY-SA covers the photo, NOT rights in the brewery's artwork/marks.

allSources.name: U.P.C. Data 4 Beverage Alcohol
allSources.license: proprietary database license (commercial, no share-alike)
allSources.coverage: 150,000+ beer/wine/spirits records, manufacturer-sourced; UPC+EAN indexed
allSources.quality: high — attributed, manufacturer-sourced; fields: UPC, manufacturer, brand, item, container, size, country, ABV, multipack, nutrition/allergens
allSources.notes: Best CLEAN COMMERCIAL barcode→beer source: buy under a database license, no viral share-alike, resale terms negotiable. Contact-for-pricing (formats: ASCII/Excel/Access). This is the recommended paid path vs viral-licensed OFF.

allSources.name: Untappd API
allSources.license: restrictive ToS
allSources.coverage: very large global beer + checkin dataset
allSources.quality: richest consumer beer/rating data
allSources.notes: HARD FLAG — DO NOT USE for a monetized/sold product. ToS: 'will not sell, rent, or trade any data acquired from the Untappd API'; 'You cannot use the Untappd data to build your own beer database'; cache must be deleted every 24h; no mining/analytics. Fatal for resale. (Untappd for Business is a separate paid product, not a data-licensing channel.)

allSources.name: Brandfetch Logo/Brand API
allSources.license: limited, non-transferable, non-sublicensable; 30-day cache
allSources.coverage: millions of company/brand logos incl. many breweries; free to 500k req/mo
allSources.quality: high, brand-verified logos
allSources.notes: FLAG for a SOLD product: license is non-transferable/non-sublicensable and cached content must be refreshed every 30 days; logos remain third-party property. You cannot bake logos into a resold/transferred dataset. Fine as a live runtime lookup in a hosted app you operate; not for data resale.

allSources.name: Logo.dev Logo API
allSources.license: attribution on free tier; self-host/cache on Pro+; no rights in underlying marks
allSources.coverage: broad company/brand logos; free 500k req/mo
allSources.quality: good developer-focused logo API
allSources.notes: Commercial use OK (paid tiers remove attribution/allow caching: Startup $280/yr, Pro $1,260/yr self-host/cache, Enterprise $2,800/yr custom legal). BUT like Brandfetch it grants no rights in the brewery's actual trademark/logo artwork — those remain the brewery's IP. Runtime logo display, not label-art resale.

allSources.name: Wikimedia Commons (beer labels / logos / imagery)
allSources.license: per-file: CC0/CC-BY/CC-BY-SA/PD-textlogo/threshold-of-originality
allSources.coverage: scattered beer labels, brewery logos, glassware/style imagery
allSources.quality: inconsistent; must verify each file
allSources.notes: FLAG: Commons only accepts freely-licensed/PD media and rejects fair-use, but the LABEL ARTWORK itself is copyright+trademark of the brewery. Only PD-textlogo / below-threshold-of-originality items are safe to reuse; many uploaded labels are actually copyvios. Never bulk-scrape labels. Use per-file, license-verified, for style/generic imagery or confirmed PD marks.

allSources.name: Beer Maverick (hops & yeast reference)
allSources.license: proprietary; API internal-only
allSources.coverage: 300+ hops, largest online yeast DB; substitutes, aroma, style pairings
allSources.quality: best-in-class hop/yeast reference detail
allSources.notes: FLAG: no public/licensable API (API is internal-use only); content proprietary. Reference-only; do not scrape for resale. Use MIT HopDatabase instead for a commercial product.

allSources.name: UPCitemdb / Go-UPC / Barcode Lookup (general barcode APIs)
allSources.license: proprietary paid tiers; redistribution limited by ToS
allSources.coverage: 700M+ barcodes (UPCitemdb); beer coverage incidental/uneven
allSources.quality: broad but shallow for beer specifically
allSources.notes: Usable as a paid fallback UPC lookup, but beer attribution is thin and their ToS limit bulk redistribution/resale of the underlying DB. For a beer-specific resellable dataset, U.P.C. Data 4 Beverage Alcohol is the stronger buy.
